issue_title: Feature: Add support for key remapping
issue_body:
### Solution/Idea

As Files gets more advanced and the number of shortcuts keeps growing, it'll be useful to allow keyboard remapping to allow users to configure shortcuts to work the way they like it.

![image](https://github.com/files-community/Files/assets/39923744/f9e173ce-c229-4ee3-b08c-60f695f186c6)


### Requirement

This proposal will add a settings page called "Actions"
This page will contain a list of actions
This proposal will allow configuring the shortcut or gesture to configure each action
This proposal will allow users to reset all actions to the default

### Files Version

v2.3

### Windows Version

_No response_
pr_title: Feature: Added support for custom key bindings
pr_body:
## Summary

- [x] Added an empty page named Actions
- [x] Added PointerOver visual state
- [x] Added support to edit remapping with custom TextBox editor
- [x] Added support to save remapped keyboard to the json
- [x] Added support to remove a mapping including default ones
- [x] Added support to cancel editing
- [x] Added support to show a teaching tip says it's already taken
- [x] Added support to reset all mappings to default
- [x] Added automation test for Actions page
- [x] Localize
- [x] Hide actions that use mouse buttons (this is only temporary)
- [x] Fix issue where alt keys can switch to other pages
    ⇒ Temporarily we removed access keys of the navigation view in Settings dialog. Once we go towards Settings tabs we can add back safely without malfunctioning

## PR Checklist

- [x] Were these changes approved in an issue or discussion with the project maintainers? In order to prevent extra work, feature requests and changes to the codebase must be approved before the pull request will be reviewed. This prevents extra work for the contributors and maintainers.
   Closes #9559
- [x] Did you build the app and test your changes?
- [ ] Did you check for accessibility? You can use Accessibility Insights for this.
- [ ] Did you remove any strings from the en-us resource file?
   - [ ] Did you search the solution to see if the string is still being used? 
- [x] Did you implement any design changes to an existing feature?
   - [x] Was this change approved?
- [x] Are there any other steps that were used to validate these changes?
   1. Go to Actions page in Settings dialog
   2. Edit, reset, save a mapping
